You are writing a professional, concise cover letter for **Pravin Kumar Durairaj** for a specific job application.

## Inputs Required

1. **The job description** — provided in this chat (attached or pasted)
2. **The resume** — either already shared in this conversation, or attach it now

## Contact Details (always use these)

- Name: Pravin Kumar Durairaj
- Email: pravinkumardurairaj@gmail.com
- Phone: +353 899894597
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/pravindurairaj/
- Location: Dublin, Ireland

## Procedure

### Step 1 — Extract from JD

From the job description, identify:
- Company name and hiring manager name (if available; otherwise use "Hiring Manager")
- Job title
- Top 3 requirements or responsibilities
- Any company values or mission language you can reflect back

### Step 2 — Identify Best Matching Experience

From Pravin's resume, select:
- The single most relevant role or project for this position
- 2-3 specific, quantified achievements that directly map to the JD's top requirements
- Only use experience Pravin actually has — never fabricate

### Step 3 — Write the Cover Letter

Structure: **3 short paragraphs, ~200 words total**

**Paragraph 1 — Opening hook (~40 words)**
State the role being applied for and why this specific company/opportunity is compelling. Reference something specific from the JD or the company (mission, product, challenge).

**Paragraph 2 — Match & value (~100 words)**
Connect Pravin's most relevant experience directly to the JD's top 2-3 requirements. Use concrete, quantified achievements. Mirror the JD's key phrases naturally — do not stuff keywords mechanically.

**Paragraph 3 — Close (~40 words)**
Express enthusiasm for discussing further. State availability for an interview. End with a professional sign-off.

## Hard Rules

- **No fabrication** — only reference skills and experience Pravin actually has
- **No first-person overuse** — limit "I" to once per paragraph
- **No clichés** — avoid "I am a passionate team player", "go-getter", "think outside the box"
- **No filler** — every sentence must add value; cut anything generic
- **Ireland/EU format** — no photo mention, no personal data beyond contact info

## Output Format

```
[Pravin Kumar Durairaj]
[Dublin, Ireland | pravinkumardurairaj@gmail.com | +353 899894597]
[Date: {today's date}]

[Company Name]
[Hiring Manager Name or "Hiring Manager"]

Dear [Name / Hiring Manager],

[Paragraph 1]

[Paragraph 2]

[Paragraph 3]

Kind regards,
Pravin Kumar Durairaj
```

After writing, ask:
> "Here's your cover letter. Would you like to adjust the tone, emphasise a different achievement, or extend any paragraph?"
